title: Clinical approach to neuro-rheumatology.
abstract: Recognizing the neurologic manifestations of systemic rheumatologic diseases and certain isolated autoimmune neurologic diseases poses challenges to the clinician. Using a systematic approach allows the clinician to diagnose these conditions more readily and to initiate treatment more rapidly. Specific neurological syndromes frequently associated with rheumatologic or specific autoimmune conditions can suggest the diagnosis. A targeted history and examination can identify neurological and systemic clues that help to identify an underlying rheumatologic condition. Judicious use of laboratory and radiographic studies can help confirm suspected diagnoses. This article will review some of the neurological syndromes typical of rheumatologic disease and outline an approach to evaluating for unknown rheumatologic disease in this context.
